reason: Response A has the warm, flowing, slightly generic-but-earnest tone typical of Haiku, with longer cohesive paragraphs and full sentences that hedge toward inclusive enthusiasm ("Whether this was on your bucket list or the start of a new passion"). Response B's choppy fragments, em-dash-heavy rhetorical structure, and self-consciously punchy closers ("Well done. Seriously.") read like a larger model deliberately performing a terse, "authentic" voice rather than Haiku's natural register. The imitation in B overuses stylistic affectation that Haiku tends not to default to.
